sanskrit:
यतु कामेप्सुना कर्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः।
क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम्॥
transliteration: yat tu kämepsunä karma sähaìkäreëa vä punaù | kriyate bahuläyäsaà tad räjasam udähåtam ||
literal_meaning: But that action which is done with desire for enjoyment, or again with ego-sense, performed with much effort—that is declared to be of the nature of rajas.
contextual_meaning: Krishna flags the psychological trap of over-drive: when action is fueled by craving, identity-inflation, and strain, it keeps the doer on a treadmill of never-enough. It is passion that feels like progress yet breeds burnout.
psychological_state: Hyper-aroused striving to prove self-worth; chronic stress masked as productivity.
trigger_scenario: Pulling repeated all-nighters to hit a sales target so your performance review outshines peers, while secretly terrified you’re only as good as last quarter’s numbers.
anchor_text: Work lightly, without craving or ego.
